title: Plagiobothrys chorisianus
taxonomy: Plagiobothrys chorisianus is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is also known as desert blue star or Chorisanthe, and it is typically found in dry, sandy areas. The classification of Plagiobothrys chorisianus is as a member of the genus Plagiobothrys, within the family Boraginaceae. The species is further classified as Plagiobothrys chorisianus (Greene) I.M. Johnston.

intro: Plagiobothrys chorisianus, also known as Fiddleneck, is a flowering annual plant belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and native to the western coastal regions of North America. Fiddleneck is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, contributing to its potential in treating various health issues such as respiratory infections and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Fiddleneck include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Fiddleneck is often used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat coughs, fever, and skin conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.
